edges.note: Both govern patient-material voice and consistency across LISRAYA output.
Both define the self-contained modular section contract for assembly.
Declared background color enables the assembler to enforce dark-adjacency.
Declared consumed footnote symbols feed the assembly-time assignment ordering.
Coral restriction constrains the tertiary tier of the palette hierarchy.
The exclusivity rule permits only the tint values bound in the specified tint bindings rule.
Masthead gradient is a specific member of the approved set with a dedicated logo-mark contrast role.
Direction rule governs how approved gradients render in masthead/banner contexts.
Describes sanctioned application contexts for the approved gradient set.
Gold/Sunshine text avoidance is a specific application of the AA contrast minimums.
Governs the background asset behavior and skip fallback for the composition rule.
Adds the mandatory signature radius constraint to the callout button defined in the composition.
Geometry specifies the exact positioning for the structure defined above.
Radius spec applies to the same box and badge.
Shadow applies to the same badge element.
Fallback addresses email-client failures of the absolute-positioning geometry.
Cardinality limit applies alongside the CTA button styling binding.
Both govern the wave device construction and colorways.
Usage guidance for the same wave assets.
Photography treatment and its accent-shape crop apply together.
Consistency-of-orientation rule refines the base accent-shape radius rule for email.
Charts/tables are an explicit exception to the asymmetric accent-shape radius.
Container styling and color logic apply together within chart sections.
Headline and statistical hierarchy define the chart typography system together.
Palette restriction applies alongside the minimal line style for all icons.
Size/contrast baseline complements the style rule for the same icon element.
Both are layout principles governing visual clarity within a section.
The ® appears only when the generic name is included, formatted per generic name rule.
Both govern brand name presentation.
Type scale weights draw from the surface-specific Nunito Sans font family.
Line-height minimum applies to the body size defined in the type scale.
Hierarchy rule constrains how the type scale levels are applied.
Campaign headline is one of the permitted all-caps exceptions.
Chart headlines are a permitted all-caps exception.
Body stays sentence case, reinforcing the all-caps prohibition.
Both govern how bold and other emphasis devices are applied within sections/elements.
Companion emphasis-styling constraints covering non-bold devices.
Applies the DM first-mention pattern per standalone section in assembly context.
Email fallback font overrides the Agenda live-text default for the campaign headline.
The verbatim lockup copy accompanies the styled campaign headline.
Governs which of the three callout styles may co-occur.
Governs which of the three callout styles may co-occur.
Governs which of the three callout styles may co-occur.
Part of the same approved campaign messaging framework.
Shared approved messaging framework.
Shared approved messaging framework.
Shared approved messaging framework.
Both are required-element obligations within content sections.
